claimText: Cruise missiles are powered by jet engines for continuous flight and operate at relatively low altitudes within the Earth's atmosphere.
evidenceExcerpt: Cruise missiles are powered by jet engines for continuous flight and fly at low altitudes within the atmosphere.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing the propulsion and flight profile of cruise and ballistic missiles.
distractorHooks: Powered only during the initial boost phase

claimText: Ballistic missiles are rocket-powered only during their initial boost phase and coast unpowered for the majority of their flight.
evidenceExcerpt: Ballistic missiles are rocket-powered initially, then coast unpowered in a ballistic trajectory arcing into the atmosphere.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanics of ballistic missile flight.
distractorHooks: Fly at low altitudes to evade radar

claimText: Cruise missiles are harder to detect but easier to intercept after detection, whereas ballistic missiles are easier to detect early but harder to intercept due to their high speed.
evidenceExcerpt: Cruise missiles are harder to detect but easier to intercept after detection, whereas ballistic missiles are easier to detect early but harder to intercept due to speed.
examUse: Comparison question testing the tactical advantages and vulnerabilities of different missile types.
distractorHooks: Ballistic missiles are harder to detect early
Cruise missiles are harder to intercept after detection
Both missile types are equally easy to intercept

claimText: Quasi ballistic missiles follow a low ballistic trajectory but retain the ability to maneuver and change course during flight.
evidenceExcerpt: Quasi ballistic missiles follow a low ballistic trajectory but can maneuver and change course during flight.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question defining quasi ballistic missiles.
distractorHooks: Follow a high-altitude arcing trajectory without maneuverability
Are identical to cruise missiles in flight profile
